decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's mana base by utilizing land destruction spells like Cleansing Wildfire, Demolition Field, and Field of Ruin, while also controlling the board with removal spells like Path to Exile and Solitude. The deck also uses cards like Price of Freedom and Wrath of the Skies to generate card advantage and finish off the opponent. Elspeth, Storm Slayer provides a win condition in the form of a powerful planeswalker, while March of Otherworldly Light and The Legend of Roku offer additional versatile removal options.

The deck focuses on combining aggressive creatures with blink effects to continually pressure the opponent and disrupt their game plan. By using cards like Ephemerate and Teferi, Time Raveler to blink powerful creatures like Ketramose, the New Dawn and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, the deck can generate significant card advantage and tempo. The inclusion of disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation helps to control the game and protect the aggro plan. With a mix of removal, card draw, and disruptive elements, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly and efficiently.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays combined with burn spells and disruptive effects to quickly overwhelm opponents. The strategy involves using creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Voice of Victory to deal damage to the opponent quickly, while also utilizing cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Goblin Bombardment for additional damage and removal. The deck also includes cards like Blood Moon and Static Prison to disrupt opponent's game plan and slow them down. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to apply constant pressure and end the game quickly before opponents can stabilize.

The deck focuses on using creatures to assemble a toolbox of versatile options, allowing for a variety of combos and interactions. Agatha's Soul Cauldron and Malevolent Rumble provide control options, while Green Sun's Zenith and Spymaster's Vault help to search for specific creatures. Yawgmoth, Thran Physician acts as a powerful engine for sacrificing and drawing cards, while Walking Ballista and Delighted Halfling provide additional combo potential. Overall, the deck aims to utilize its creatures strategically to outmaneuver opponents and achieve victory through a combination of synergy and versatility.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's resources and using them to gain advantage. It utilizes cards like Dauthi Voidwalker and Necrodominance to disrupt the opponent's strategy and take control of the game. Gonti's Machinations and Sorin of House Markov provide additional ways to manipulate the game state in your favor. The deck also includes a mix of removal spells like Fatal Push and Witherbloom Charm to handle threats efficiently. Overall, the deck aims to outlast the opponent by outvaluing them and dominating the game through resource management and control.

The deck focuses on using artifacts, like Mishra's Bauble and Mox Opal, to generate mana and power up creatures like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary. Emry, Lurker of the Loch allows for recursion of artifacts while cards like Arcbound Ravager and Weapons Manufacturing can quickly boost damage output. Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke provide control options against opponents, while Urza's Saga can search for key artifacts. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a mix of aggro creatures and artifact synergy.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and efficiently bring powerful creatures back from the graveyard to the battlefield by using cards like Unearth, Persist, and Faithless Looting. This allows the player to overwhelm their opponent with high-impact creatures such as Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones. The deck also includes disruption cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the game and protect the combo pieces. By carefully managing resources and setting up the graveyard, the player aims to execute their combo strategy and win the game.

This UW Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy while generating card advantage and controlling the board. It utilizes counterspells like Counterspell and Dovin's Veto to protect its game plan, along with removal spells such as Prismatic Ending and Solitude. The deck includes powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Time Raveler to control the tempo of the game and generate value. Memory Deluge and Meticulous Archive help dig through the deck for key cards, while cards like Flooded Strand and Field of Ruin provide mana fixing and disruption. Overall, the deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver opponents through careful planning and resource management.

This deck focuses on using low-cost creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent and deal damage efficiently. The main strategy is to control the board with cards like Leyline Binding and Leyline of the Guildpact while deploying aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Territorial Kavu to pressure the opponent. Quantum Riddler helps with card advantage and consistency, while cards like Lightning Bolt and Stubborn Denial provide additional removal and protection. Teferi, Time Raveler offers disruption and tempo advantage, making it difficult for opponents to effectively respond to the deck's threats.

This deck focuses on aggressive play with high synergy between creatures and disruption spells. It aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly with a combination of efficient creatures like Ragavan and Guide of Souls, backed up by removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Lightning Bolt. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Boromir, Warden of the Tower provide additional support and utility. The deck also includes cards like Goblin Bombardment and Thraben Charm for added versatility and reach. Overall, the deck seeks to maintain pressure on the opponent while disrupting their game plan to secure victory.

This deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and direct damage spells, utilizing powerful mana ramp and control elements to push ahead. The Boros color combination allows for a blend of efficient creatures and burn spells that can quickly take control of the game. The key strategy is to apply constant pressure on the opponent while using removal and counter spells to protect your creatures and maintain board presence. By combining speed, power, and disruption, this deck aims to close out the game quickly before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's mana base through land destruction spells like Cleansing Wildfire and Field of Ruin while also controlling the board with removal spells like Path to Exile and Wrath of God. It aims to maintain control of the game with powerful planeswalkers like Chandra, Torch of Defiance and finish off the opponent with threats like Solitude and Price of Freedom. The deck also has a small amount of card advantage and card selection to ensure a steady stream of threats and answers.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling a combo involving Goblin Charbelcher, using mana rituals and card draw spells to generate enough mana to activate its ability and deal lethal damage to the opponent. Cards like Irencrag Feat and Orim's Chant help protect the combo, while Legion Leadership and Pinnacle Monk provide additional utility and support. The deck is designed to win the game without relying on traditional land cards, making it a unique and powerful combo deck in the Modern format.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly discard big creatures into the graveyard with cards like Faithless Looting, Persist, and Thoughtseize, and then bring them back onto the battlefield with cards like Unearth and Archon of Cruelty. The deck aims to use these powerful reanimated creatures to overwhelm the opponent and win the game quickly, before they have a chance to respond. The strategy relies on a combination of combo pieces and efficient removal spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan and secure victory.

This deck focuses on quickly playing artifacts and utilizing cards that benefit from having artifacts on the battlefield. The strategy involves playing cheap artifacts to generate mana and keep the pressure on your opponent. Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Mishra's Bauble help with card advantage while Urza's Saga can tutor for key artifacts. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays and efficient use of artifacts to control the battlefield and win quickly.

The deck focuses on assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Mine, Power Plant, and Tower) to generate a large amount of mana quickly. This mana is then used to play powerful Eldrazi creatures such as Emrakul, the Promised End and Thought-Knot Seer, backed up by supporting cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storm. The deck aims to control the game by disrupting the opponent's strategy while building up its own board presence and eventually overpowering them with huge creatures and powerful abilities.
